article_title: CISA Adds SimpleHelp, Samsung MagicINFO, and End-of-Life D-Link Flaws to KEV Catalog as DragonForce Ransomware and Mirai Botnets Exploit Them in the Wild

content_preview.title: CISA Adds SimpleHelp, Samsung MagicINFO, and End-of-Life D-Link Flaws to KEV Catalog as DragonForce Ransomware and Mirai Botnets Exploit Them in the Wild
content_preview.summary: CISA's April 24 KEV update flags four actively exploited vulnerabilities tied to ransomware against managed service providers and Mirai DDoS botnets, with a May 8 federal patching deadline.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency added four vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog on April 24, 2026, citing confirmed in-the-wild exploitation across remote management software, digital signage servers, and discontinued consumer routers. The additions cover two SimpleHelp flaws being chained by the DragonForce ransomware operation, a Samsung MagicINFO 9 path-traversal bug, and a command-injection vulnerability in end-of-life D-Link D...

editor_notes.content_quality: Substantial cybersecurity News piece (1,199 words). Bot followed the project memory pattern and cited CISA + NVD as direct primary sources for 4 of 7 sources. All major claims verified verbatim.

source_verification.https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/alerts/: source-0.html — April 24 alert verifies all 4 CVEs (CVE-2024-57726, CVE-2024-57728, CVE-2024-7399, CVE-2025-29635), BOD 22-01 framing.
source_verification.https://www.cisa.gov/known-exploited-vul: source-1.html — Live KEV catalog snapshot confirms dateAdded 2026-04-24 and dueDate 2026-05-08 verbatim for the entries.
source_verification.NVD entries (sources 2-4): All three CVE NVD entries verified: CVE-2024-57726 9.9 missing-authorization in SimpleHelp 5.5.7; CVE-2024-7399 dual CVSS (Samsung 8.8 / NIST 9.8) in MagicINFO 9 < 21.1050; CVE-2025-29635 7.2 command injection in DIR-823X via /goform/set_prohibiting POST.
source_verification.https://thehackernews.com/2026/04/cisa-a: source-5.html — DragonForce attribution + Field Effect/Sophos vendor reports verified.
source_verification.https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/se: source-6.html — Akamai SIRT detection, tuxnokill Mirai variant, dlink.sh script, November 2024 EOL all verified.

editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All quantitative claims (CVE IDs, CVSS scores, version cutoffs, dates, exploitation attribution) verified verbatim across CISA + NVD + secondary outlets.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral, technical. Honest about limitations (KEV doesn't quantify exploitation, ransomware attribution is from third-party vendor reports not CISA itself).
editor_notes.originality: Distinct from prior KEV coverage (Marimo, Windows Shell + ConnectWise) — different threat ecosystem (RaaS supply chain + Mirai botnets + EOL hardware).
editor_notes.overall_assessment: APPROVE. Excellent CISA + NVD primary-source citations following the established memory pattern. All claims verified verbatim.
